channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Medicaid puzzle confronts Minnesota lawmakers. Federal cuts could hit health safety net program
item.description: Republicans in Washington are working on slashing the federal budget to help pay for a slate of tax breaks. State lawmakers know it could mean a health care funding hole, but they don’t know how much they’ll have to backfill.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: In Pelican Rapids, teens learn the skill of civil discourse
item.description: In a deeply polarized country, a new program in Minnesota is teaching middle and high schoolers how to find common ground in their communities. Program leaders say it’s an effort to inspire young people to become engaged politically and in their towns.
item.dc:creator: Catharine Richert, MPR News

item.title: Bill O'Reilly believes Trump has several tariff deals already done
item.description: Bill O'Reilly recently spoke with President Trump and believes the president has several tariff deals already done.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Despite a court order, White House bars AP from Oval Office event
item.description: Despite a court order, a reporter and photographer from The Associated Press were barred from an Oval Office news conference on Monday with President Donald Trump and his counterpart from El Salvador, Nayib Bukele.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE uncovers massive VA contract for ‘salary survey data and analysis'
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) on Monday announced more cuts within the Department of Veterans Affairs by millions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Female soldier becomes the first woman to compete in the grueling Army Ranger contest
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A female Army Ranger for the first time competed in the annual Best Ranger Competition, and her two-soldier team finished the grueling three-day event over the weekend and came in 14th overall. First Lt. Gabrielle White, and her teammate, Capt.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former President Joe Biden to speak in Chicago on Tuesday in effort to rally support for Social Security
item.description: Former President Joe Biden is scheduled to speak at the national conference of Advocates, Counselors and Representatives for the Disabled.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: Harvard won’t comply with Trump admin’s demands amid threats of cutting federal funding
item.description: Harvard University President Alan M. Garber assured the community that the university would not comply with the Trump administration's threats to cut federal funding.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: After loss to Slotkin, Michigan Republican Mike Rogers launches another Senate bid
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Former U.S. Rep. Mike Rogers is launching another Republican bid for the U.S. Senate in Michigan, following a narrow loss to Democratic Sen. Elissa Slotkin last year in a hotly contested open race. This time, Rogers is vying for the seat being vacated by Democratic Sen.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gen. Caine has taken over as chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Air Force Gen. Dan Caine has been sworn in as chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff after a flurry of paperwork was finished to allow him to fill the job nearly two months after President Donald Trump fired his predecessor.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cuomo lands key endorsement as comeback bid for NYC mayor heats up
item.description: Former New York Gov. Andrew Cuomo is showcasing the backing of two powerful labor unions as he runs for mayor of the nation's most populous city.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pete Marocco, mastermind behind dismantling of USAID projects, leaves State Department
item.description: Pete Marocco, who led the dismantling of USAID under Secretary of State Marco Rubio, departs as critics accuse him of chaos and foreign aid cuts that gutted U.S. global development programs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Minneapolis council member Emily Koski drops out of mayor's race
item.description: Minneapolis City Council member Emily Koski has dropped out of the mayor’s race, according to a campaign update Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Trump pharma tariff threat raises specter of shortages, price hikes
item.description: The pharmaceutical industry is bracing for chaos if President Trump follows through on his threat to impose "a major tariff" on prescription drug imports.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Sanders jokingly calls Ocasio-Cortez his daughter at Utah rally
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) joked during a joint appearance at a Utah rally on Sunday that Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) was his daughter, praising the progressive lawmaker and highlighting her background.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Plans to celebrate America's 250th anniversary were underway. Then came the federal funding cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Community celebrations being planned to commemorate the nation's 250th anniversary next year are at risk of being significantly scaled back or canceled because of federal funding cuts under President Donald Trump's administration, according to multiple state humanities councils acr
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH and GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro and the alleged arson at his official residence
item.description: A man is facing charges after authorities say he broke into the Pennsylvania governor’s mansion in the middle of the night, set a fire that left significant damage and forced Gov. Josh Shapiro, his family and guests to evacuate the building during the Jewish holiday of Passover.
item.dc:creator: Marc Levy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: President Trump says CBS and '60 Minutes' should 'pay a big price' for going after him
item.description: President Donald Trump bitterly attacked “60 Minutes” shortly after the CBS newsmagazine broadcast stories on Ukraine and Greenland on Sunday, saying the network was out of control and should “pay a big price” for going after him.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Military schools offer test case for Trump education reforms
item.description: Military academies could increasingly show what President Trump wants to see from public schools and colleges.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota Senate Minority Leader Johnson is optimistic lawmakers will make session deadline
item.description: State lawmakers have a big hill to climb with just over a month before the Legislative session is over. Despite economic uncertainty, Senate Minority Leader Mark Johnson, R-East Grand Forks, said he’s optimistic they’ll get a budget passed before the deadline.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Lukas Levin, and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Trump tariffs bring Democrat disagreements to surface
item.description: President Donald Trump’s tariffs are putting Democrats in a challenging political position as they decide how strongly to denounce his trade policy.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Trump: Russia-Ukraine talks at 'put up or shut up' point
item.description: Trump's comments came after a top envoy traveled to Russia to meet directly with President Vladimir Putin on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Maher praises ‘gracious and measured’ Trump after White House visit
item.description: The talk show host emphasized that he did not turn “MAGA” during the meeting over dinner at the White House on Mar. 31.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration says it will exclude some electronics from 'reciprocal' tariffs
item.description: The Trump administration says it will exclude electronics like smartphones and laptops from ‘reciprocal’ tariffs, a move that could help keep the prices down for popular consumer electronics that aren’t usually made in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: China put steep tariffs on U.S. exports. Farmers are worried
item.description: The first Trump administration spent $28 billion bailing out farmers during a trade war with China. The White House has said it's starting to look at how to help this time around.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: Trump eyes major cuts to NOAA research
item.description: The Trump administration is eyeing cuts to climate, weather and ocean research in a draft budget blueprint for the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Stocks slide as Trump's trade war with China spooks Wall Street
item.description: Stocks slid Thursday as President Trump’s escalating trade war with China and concerns about its economic implications continued to rattle Wall Street.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump reverses Biden rule restricting water usage for showerheads and other home appliances
item.description: President Donald Trump has long complained about modern rules that limit water flow for showerheads, making it harder for him to wash his "beautiful hair.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rep. Ilhan Omar will run for reelection in the U.S. House, not an open Minnesota Senate seat
item.description: U.S. Rep. Ilhan Omar had been considering a run for the seat currently held by Sen. Tina Smith. The DFLer will instead seek a fifth House term.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: New York bill aims to help homeless people and their pets stay together
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – A new state bill aims to expand access to co-sheltering, allowing homeless people and domestic abuse survivors to stay together with their pets during a housing crisis. Senate Bill S673, sponsored by New York state Sen. Monica R.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, PIX 11
